segments.text: And it came to pass, that when Isaac was old, and his eyes were dim, so that he could not see, he called Esau his eldest son,
and said unto him,
My son:
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Behold, here am I.
segments.speaker: Esau

segments.text: Behold now, I am old, I know not the day of my death:
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Now therefore take, I pray thee, thy weapons, thy quiver and thy bow, and go out to the field, and take me some venison;
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: And make me savoury meat, such as I love, and bring it to me, that I may eat; that my soul may bless thee before I die.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: And Rebekah heard when Isaac spake to Esau his son. And Esau went to the field to hunt for venison, and to bring it.
And Rebekah spake unto Jacob her son,
Behold, I heard thy father speak unto Esau thy brother, saying,
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: Bring me venison, and make me savoury meat, that I may eat, and bless thee before the LORD before my death.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Bring me venison, and make me savoury meat, that I may eat, and bless thee before the LORD before my death.
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice according to that which I command thee.
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: Go now to the flock, and fetch me from thence two good kids of the goats; and I will make them savoury meat for thy father, such as he loveth:
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: And thou shalt bring it to thy father, that he may eat, and that he may bless thee before his death.
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: Behold, Esau my brother is a hairy man, and I am a smooth man:
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: My father peradventure will feel me, and I shall seem to him as a deceiver; and I shall bring a curse upon me, and not a blessing.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Upon me be thy curse, my son: only obey my voice, and go fetch me them.
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: And he came unto his father,
My father:
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Here am I; who art thou, my son?
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: I am Esau thy firstborn; I have done according as thou badest me: arise, I pray thee, sit and eat of my venison, that thy soul may bless me.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: And it came to pass, as soon as Isaac had made an end of blessing Jacob, and Jacob was yet scarce gone out from the presence of Isaac his father, that Esau his brother came in from his hunting.
And Esau hated Jacob because of the blessing wherewith his father blessed him:
The days of mourning for my father are at hand; then will I slay my brother Jacob.
segments.speaker: Esau

segments.text: And these words of Esau her elder son were told to Rebekah:
Behold, thy brother Esau, as touching thee, doth comfort himself, purposing to kill thee.
segments.speaker: Rebekah
